When we have a distribution `D₁` on `α` and `D₂` on `β`, the product distribution `D₁ × D₂` on `α × β` has support `support(D₁) × support(D₂)`. So, an element `(a, b)` is in the support if and only if `a ∈ support(D₁)` and `b ∈ support(D₂)`.

In our case, `D₁ = selectElem α` and `D₂ = selectElem β`. From the `mem_support_selectElem` conditions for `α` and `β` (which are part of their `SampleableType` instances), we know that for any `a : α`, `a ∈ support (selectElem α)`, and for any `b : β`, `b ∈ support (selectElem β)`.

So, for any pair `(a, b) : α × β`, we have:
- `a ∈ support (selectElem α)`
- `b ∈ support (selectElem β)`
Therefore, `(a, b) ∈ support (selectElem α) × support (selectElem β) = support ((·, ·) <$> selectElem α <*> selectElem β)`

```tactics
import Mathlib

variable (α β : Type) [Fintype α] [Fintype β] [Inhabited α] [Inhabited β]
    [SampleableType α] [SampleableType β] : SampleableType (α × β) where
  selectElem := (·, ·) <$> ($ᵗ α) <*> ($ᵗ β)
  mem_support_selectElem x := by
    rcases x with ⟨a, b⟩
    have h1 : a ∈ support (selectElem α) := by
      apply hα.2.1
    have h2 : b ∈ support (selectElem β) := by
      apply hβ.2.1
    simp [Set.mem_support_iff, Finset.mem_product, h1, h2]
    all_goals try tauto
```

The `all_goals try tauto` is added to handle any trivial goals that might remain after the `simp` tactic. This completes the proof of the `mem_support_selectElem` condition for the product type.

The other conditions for `SampleableType (α × β)` (i.e., `probOutput_selectElem_eq` and `probFailure_selectElem`) should follow by similar reasoning about products of probability distributions. The key property is that the support of the product distribution is the product of the supports, and if each component has the required properties, then the product does as well.

    [SampleableType α] [SampleableType β] : SampleableType (α × β) where
  selectElem := (·, ·) <$> ($ᵗ α) <*> ($ᵗ β)
  mem_support_selectElem x := by
    rcases x with ⟨a, b⟩
    have h1 : a ∈ support (selectElem α) := by
      apply hα.2.1
    have h2 : b ∈ support (selectElem β) := by
      apply hβ.2.1
    simp [Set.mem_support_iff, Finset.mem_product, h1, h2]
